rationale: Postcondition (spec_props ==> det_values_agree_equal(r1,r2)) is fundamentally unprovable. The spec of values_agree only constrains ret.1 via `!ret.0 ==> ret.1 == E`, so when ret.0 is true the 'almost' bit is unconstrained. A witness with r1=(true,true) and r2=(true,false) satisfies all four spec conjuncts (forall_A holds, both vacuous implications hold) yet falsifies det_values_agree_equal. Tried: bare assert(r1.0==r2.0), case split on !r1.0 (inner asserts pass), trigger-discharge assertions. None can close the r1.0==true case without forbidden assume().
